decisions.title: Notion remains canonical
decisions.detail: The Netlify book displays summaries and snapshots; it does not replace Notion as the operating system.

decisions.title: Vertical book format
decisions.detail: Mobile browsing is the priority; desktop should expand modestly but preserve the book-object feeling.

decisions.title: v0.8+ visual language
decisions.detail: Late-1980s adventure-game pixel interface with Loom / King's Quest-style dithering.

decisions.title: Self Love / Clark Gable leads rollout
decisions.detail: Lead release stays focused on September 2026 with current July/August prep.

decisions.title: Public/private boundary
decisions.detail: Decide which Notion-derived data can appear in the deployed interface.

decisions.title: Dashboard beauty vs function
decisions.detail: Keep expanding functionality while restoring the visual quality of the original book mockup.

filesAssets.name: GitHub source repo

filesAssets.name: Netlify live site
filesAssets.detail: Hosted interactive book and review layer.

filesAssets.name: Visual + Zine Assets
filesAssets.detail: Book, zine, moodboard, typography, and artifact direction.

filesAssets.name: Audio + Listening
filesAssets.detail: Mix review, listening notes, playback context, and unresolved audio decisions.

filesAssets.name: Dropbox / source files
filesAssets.detail: Long-term file host and source-file archive.

openQuestions.title: How public should the portal be?
openQuestions.detail: Choose public, private, or split public/private builds before exposing real Notion links.

openQuestions.title: How much dashboard depth belongs here?
openQuestions.detail: The portal should be useful, but still feel like a book/artifact instead of a generic admin dashboard.

openQuestions.title: What should the next live data source be?
openQuestions.detail: Best candidates: Tracks, Release Calendar, Decision Log, or Files / Assets.

nextActions: Keep vertical mobile-first layout as default.
